task_completion.description: Verify that a new CDB lookup list file is created in the Wazuh lists directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for terminal commands (e.g., ls, touch, nano, vim) or a file manager showing a new file created in /var/ossec/etc/lists/.

task_completion.description: Verify that the CDB list contains at least 10 CVE IDs extracted from the CISA KEV catalog.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the contents of the newly created CDB list file (via cat, less, or a text editor) showing multiple lines starting with 'CVE-YYYY-NNNN'.

task_completion.description: Verify that a custom Wazuh detection rule is created that references the new CDB list.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for an XML file being edited (typically in /var/ossec/etc/rules/) containing a <rule> block with a <list> tag that explicitly references the filename of the newly created CDB list.

task_completion.description: Verify that the Wazuh vulnerability detection module (syscollector/wodle) is configured in ossec.conf.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for /var/ossec/etc/ossec.conf open in a text editor or being modified via terminal, specifically showing the <vulnerability-detector> or <syscollector> blocks being enabled or configured.

task_completion.description: Verify that a threat intelligence integration report of sufficient length is created on the Desktop.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a file named kev_integration_report.txt on the Desktop (/home/ga/Desktop/) open in a text editor or displayed in the terminal, containing a substantial paragraph of text (at least ~60 words / 600 characters).

integrity.description: Verify that the agent actually extracted data from the provided CISA KEV JSON file rather than hallucinating CVEs.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for commands like jq, grep, cat, or a script reading /tmp/cisa_kev.json to extract the CVE IDs.

integrity.description: Verify that the integration report contains actual threat intelligence context and is not just a copy-paste of the task description.
integrity.visual_evidence: Read the visible text of kev_integration_report.txt to ensure it reads like a genuine summary or report rather than a repetition of the prompt instructions.

integrity.description: Verify that Wazuh configuration files were edited in their actual system paths and not mocked in a local user directory.
integrity.visual_evidence: Check the file paths in text editor title bars or terminal prompts to ensure edits are happening in /var/ossec/etc/ and not /home/ga/ or /tmp/.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The CDB list must be created in /var/ossec/etc/lists/ and contain at least 10 CVE IDs. The source data for these CVEs is located at /tmp/cisa_kev.json. The integration report must be saved exactly to /home/ga/Desktop/kev_integration_report.txt and contain at least 600 characters (approximately 60 words).
